tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Apple is one of the most widely cultivated fruits in the world and is known for its sweet and crisp taste.

tab2.title: Nutrition
tab2.content: Apples contain fiber, vitamin C and various antioxidants that are beneficial for health.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: The phrase 'An apple a day keeps the doctor away' highlights its health benefits.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Bananas are one of the most popular fruits worldwide and are known for their soft texture and sweetness.

tab2.title: Nutrition
tab2.content: Bananas are rich in potassium, vitamin B6 and dietary fiber.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Bananas are technically classified as berries in botanical terms.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Mango is known as the 'King of Fruits' and is famous for its sweet and juicy flavor.

tab2.title: Nutrition
tab2.content: Mangoes contain vitamins A, C and antioxidants.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: India is the largest producer of mangoes in the world.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Strawberries are small red fruits known for their sweet flavor and aroma.

tab2.title: Nutrition
tab2.content: They are rich in vitamin C, antioxidants and fiber.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Strawberries are the only fruit with seeds on the outside.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Pineapple is a tropical fruit known for its sweet and tangy taste.

tab2.title: Nutrition
tab2.content: It contains vitamin C and bromelain, an enzyme that aids digestion.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Pineapple plants take around two years to produce a single fruit.
